comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Feature complete and validated by the full test suite (2468 passing), a 4-round adversarial design review (APPROVE), prior-art validation, and a live gated-close+signing demo. A monolithic whole-epic graph=True LLM verification exceeds the practical step budget (a documented limitation); child workstreams WS-1..5 are closed and individually verified.". Session: 86ef8e05.

compiled_state.description:
LLM completion-verification close gate: an optional, signed agentic gate that verifies a ticket's completion criteria are met before closure.

## Context
rebar ships an optional LLM agent-operations framework (`rebar.llm`: review_ticket / review_code / scan_epics_for_spec) over library/CLI/MCP, plus an environment-bound HMAC signing module (`rebar.signing`: sign_manifest / verify_signature, with key-fingerprint attribution and HEAD binding) and an opt-in story/epic close `_signature_gate`. This epic adds a **completion-verifier** agent (adapted from the DSO `completion-verifier`) that, on a close attempt, verifies every acceptance/success/close/completion criterion (and, for bugs, that the bug is resolved) is demonstrably met, then — on PASS — produces a **signed** completion attestation that the close gate certifies.

Trust model (per design): security is only meaningful when rebar runs as the MCP server, whose environment signing key is canonical; CLI/library installs sign with a local key that CI reads as `foreign_key` (accepted-as-insecure by design). CI verifies the attestation under the MCP server's key. `--force-close` closes the ticket but withholds the signature, so a closed-without-signature ticket is the durable signal that validation did not pass.

This is distinct from (but composes with) epic 8a1c (cryptographic gate hardening / protected tags).

## What changes
- A pluggable per-operation structured-output **contract** (the runner stops hardcoding the findings model; each op/workflow selects its contract via `output_schema`).
- A `completion_verdict` schema + contract: `{verdict: PASS|FAIL, findings[], summary}` where each FAIL finding cites the failing criterion, an explanation, and (if applicable) a source-code citation.
- A `completion-verifier` reviewer prompt covering epics/stories/tasks/bugs (read-only fs + read-only rebar ticket access).
- `verify_completion` operation over library/CLI/MCP (MCP gated by `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM`).
- A close-time gate (`verify.require_completion_verification_for_close`, default off) that runs the verifier OUTSIDE the write lock, blocks on FAIL/missing-LLM (fail-closed, `--force-close` bypass), and signs the verdict on PASS.
- Enable the gate + LLM module in THIS project's rebar config.

## Success Criteria
- Closing a task/story/epic/bug with the gate on runs the verifier; PASS attaches a certified signature and the close proceeds; FAIL blocks with findings.
- `--force-close="reason"` closes without running the verifier and without a signature (no signed confirmation of passing validation).
- The optionality contract still holds: `import rebar` / `import rebar.llm` pull no heavy deps; a missing `[agents]` extra degrades cleanly on every interface.
- Each operation/workflow can declare its own output contract; findings remains the default; `completion_verdict` is the first alternate.
- This project's config enables the gate and the LLM module.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pluggable output contract: runner builds the structured-output model from `RunRequest`/step `output_schema` (default = findings), registry-based and tested.
- [ ] `completion_verdict.schema.json` added + registered in the schemas registry; Pydantic mirror pinned by a test.
- [ ] `completion-verifier` reviewer prompt added (catalog + packaged `*.md`); handles epic/story/task/bug; bug → "resolved" check.
- [ ] `verify_completion` exposed on library (`rebar.llm.verify_completion`), CLI (`rebar verify-completion`), and gated MCP (`verify_completion`).
- [ ] Close gate wired into `transition_compute` (outside lock, lazy import): FAIL or missing-LLM blocks (fail-closed) with `--force-close` bypass; PASS signs via `sign_manifest` then closes; force-close skips verify+sign.
- [ ] `verify.require_completion_verification_for_close` typed config flag (default off) with coercion + tests.
- [ ] This project's `.rebar/config.conf` enables the gate + `mcp.allow_llm`.
- [ ] Docs updated (docs/llm-framework.md + CLAUDE.md tool set); optionality + schema-pin + gate-behavior + fake-runner tests pass.
compiled_state.title: LLM completion-verification close gate (signed, optional)
